methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Bone AI, a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by DK Lee, has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety. The peer set spans seed through Series A stages within the defense and security technology sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $12M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $30M. Peer-Median analysis references four same-sector comparable companies: Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed), Ares Industries at $150M (Series A), Vatn Systems at $300M (Series A), and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A). The median across these four peers is $225M, establishing an upper bound range of $60M–$500M.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $26M–$259M, with a median of $128M. The wide spread reflects structural tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors to current capital raised, and the peer-median approach, which incorporates later-stage multiples. Key uncertainties include the relevance of Series A comparables to a seed-stage asset, and variation in defense contractor valuations tied to contract maturity and customer concentration.
